name: Resveratrol
effects: Human clinical outcome benefits remain uncertain
contraindications: High doses may cause GI effects
Use caution with anticoagulants and CYP-sensitive medicines
description: Trans-resveratrol is the main supplemental isomer; human outcome evidence is inconsistent.
summary: Grape-derived polyphenol with speculative anti-aging/cardiometabolic claims and low bioavailability.
mechanism: Proposed SIRT1-related and vascular/oxidative signaling effects; clinical significance is uncertain.
safetyNotes: GI upset increases at higher doses.
interactions: May affect CYP2C9/CYP3A4 metabolism and anticoagulant-sensitive contexts.
dosage: No RDA; trial doses commonly ~150–1000 mg/day.
preparation: Oral capsules; specialized formulations aim to improve absorption.
regulatoryStatus: Sold as dietary supplement; no drug approval.
